summary: Decompiled sysdolphin/baselib/debug.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.c, replacing the assembly object in obj_files.mk. The new C covers the debug/assert/panic path around lbl_8038815C, func_803881E4, __assert, and HSD_Panic, including OSReport, OSSaveContext, OSPanic, callback globals, and an inferred struct around lbl_80400430. The PR also added an ldscript FORCEACTIVE block for lbl_804C28D0 because #pragma force_active did not keep the symbol from being stripped.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.s, removing 158 lines of hand-written assembly/data/bss/sdata declarations for the debug module.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.c with 75 lines implementing lbl_8038815C, func_803881E4, __assert, and HSD_Panic in C.
Updated obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.s.o.
Declared callback-like extern globals lbl_804D7710, lbl_804D7714, and lbl_804D7718 and preserved behavior where lbl_8038815C optionally calls lbl_804D7710 before forwarding to lbl_804D7718.
Introduced struct UnkStruct80400430 with padding to model fields at offsets 0x52 and 0x84, allowing func_803881E4 to set lbl_80400430.unk84 to lbl_8038815C and clear lbl_80400430.unk52.
Moved assembly data into C equivalents: OSContext lbl_804C2608, static empty string lbl_804D6010, and char lbl_804C28D0[0x10].
Added a static asm myStrippedFunction with four nops, documented as required to reproduce early CodeWarrior peephole behavior after stripping.
Added a FORCEACTIVE block in ldscript.lcf for lbl_804C28D0 because the source comment says #pragma force_active was not sufficient to prevent Metrowerks from stripping the symbol.
smart_moves: Used a minimal padded placeholder struct for lbl_80400430 rather than over-naming unknown fields, while still exposing the two offsets needed for matching behavior.
Kept unknown global labels such as lbl_804D7710, lbl_804D7714, lbl_804D7718, lbl_80400430, lbl_804C2608, lbl_804C28D0, and lbl_804D6010 instead of inventing unsupported semantic names.
Preserved the callback trampoline behavior in lbl_8038815C, including dereferencing arg2 for the optional lbl_804D7710 call and returning 0 after invoking lbl_804D7718.
Matched the assert/panic strings directly in C: "assertion \"%s\" failed" and "%s in %s on line %d.\n".
Used HSD_Panic from __assert and implemented HSD_Panic to save OSContext, report diagnostic text, call the optional panic callback, then fall through to OSPanic.
Documented and worked around a toolchain/linker retention problem instead of silently relying on a pragma that did not work for lbl_804C28D0.
Added a stripped static asm function solely for matching/toolchain side effects, showing attention to CodeWarrior codegen quirks beyond the visible callable functions.
decomp_lessons: When replacing a .s file with C, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the build switches from asm object to src object.
For partially understood global structs, use explicitly sized padding and offset-based field names to match layout without overclaiming semantics.
A symbol can require linker-script FORCEACTIVE even when #pragma force_active on appears in the source; verify retention in the final link/map rather than assuming the pragma worked.
Small stripped or unused functions can still affect CodeWarrior peephole/codegen behavior; preserving a local static asm stub may be necessary for matching.
Debug/assert/panic code often has varargs-sensitive calls such as OSReport and OSPanic; preserve argument order and string literals exactly.
If a function pointer global may be NULL, mirror the assembly's NULL checks before callback invocation or default initialization.
Do not rename unlabeled globals unless there is clear evidence; this PR retained lbl_* names for uncertain callbacks and data.
naming_conventions: Used lbl_ names for unknown data and callbacks where semantics were not established: lbl_804D7710, lbl_804D7714, lbl_804D7718, lbl_80400430, lbl_804C2608, lbl_804C28D0, lbl_804D6010.
Used UnkStruct80400430 for an inferred unknown struct anchored to the global symbol lbl_80400430.
Used field names unk52 and unk84 to indicate known offsets but unknown semantic meaning.
Kept exported function labels lbl_8038815C and func_803881E4 where better names were not evidenced.
Used descriptive local placeholder myStrippedFunction for the intentionally stripped static asm helper, with comments explaining its matching role.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the full debug.s assembly implementation after matching it with C.
Modeled lbl_80400430 offsets with filler arrays: filler0[0x52], unk52, unk53[0x84 - 0x53], and function pointer unk84.
Represented the former .bss OSContext allocation at lbl_804C2608 as OSContext lbl_804C2608.
Represented the former empty sdata bytes at lbl_804D6010 as static char lbl_804D6010[1] = "".
Used #pragma push, #pragma force_active on, and #pragma pop around lbl_804C28D0, then supplemented it with an ldscript FORCEACTIVE entry when the pragma did not retain the symbol.
Inserted a static asm function containing four nops to trigger the required early CodeWarrior peephole-off behavior despite the function being stripped.
Preserved function pointer calls through globals, including the optional panic hook lbl_804D7714(OSContext*, ...).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_8038815C, func_803881E4, lbl_80400430, or the lbl_804D7710/lbl_804D7714/lbl_804D7718 callback globals.
Search for other uses of ldscript FORCEACTIVE to preserve symbols when #pragma force_active fails.
Look for later cleanup of UnkStruct80400430 or identification of the field at offset 0x84.
Search for myStrippedFunction or other stripped static asm helpers used to reproduce CodeWarrior peephole behavior.
